'''Shaun Kenny-Dowall''' (born 23 January 1988) is a [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand international]] former [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|ce}} and {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]]. He was also the club captain. 

He previously played for the [[Sydney Roosters]], winning the [[2013 NRL Grand Final]] with them and the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[NRL]]. He has played for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] and [[NRL All Stars team|NRL All Stars]], and played in New Zealand's [[2010 Rugby League Four Nations|2010 Four Nations]]-winning side as well as the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]]-winning side.

==Early years==
Kenny-Dowall was born in Woodridge, [[Brisbane, Queensland]], Australia. He is of [[English people|English]] & [[Māori people|Māori]] descent specifically the  [[Ngāti Ākarana]] [[Iwi]] (tribe).

He moved to [[Auckland]] as a three-year-old and began his rugby league career playing for the East Coast Bays Barracudas on Auckland's North Shore. Kenny-Dowall later resided in [[Ngāruawāhia]], where he attended [[Ngaruawahia High School]] and played for Turangawaewae and the Ngaruawahia Panthers. Kenny-Dowall was a [[New Zealand Warriors]] junior but was unwanted by the club before moving to Sydney in 2004.<ref name="NZ_Herald_10717812">{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10717812&ref=rss|title=NRL: The Kiwi boy who joined the elite with a $1.8m deal|author=Deane, Steve|date=8 April 2011|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=9 November 2011}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall was selected for the [[Junior Kiwis]] in 2006.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://leagueunlimited.com/news/junior-kiwis-prepare-for-2006-campaign/|title=Junior Kiwis Prepare For 2006 Campaign|publisher=League Unlimited|date=2006-10-10|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref>

His father, [[John Dowall]], won a gold medal in javelin and a silver medal in shot put at the [[2000 Paralympic Games]].<ref>{{cite web| url=http://www.mailtimes.com.au/story/1822555/father-inspires-skds-shot-at-glory/| title=Father inspires SKD's shot at glory| publisher=mailtimes.com.au| date=6 October 2013| access-date=23 July 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=Walshaw|first=Nick|date=18 September 2010|title=Rise and rise of backpacker SKD|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/rise-and-rise-of-backpacker-skd/news-story/de4a8bbf386483b858ffdc39a04e8436?sv=e77482c11c4600a57d56dbb6176d3c46|publisher=Courier Mail|access-date=22 February 2018}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===2006===
Kenny-Dowall played in the 2006 [[NSW Cup]] grand final for [[Newtown Jets|Newtown]] who were the [[Sydney Roosters]] feeder club at the time against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]].  Newtown would lose the grand final 20-19 at [[Stadium Australia]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.newtownjets.com/pastSeasons/2007-news/2006GrandFinal.php/|title=2006 Grand Final - Newtown Jets|website=www.newtownjets.com|access-date=7 May 2021|archive-date=7 May 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210507183424/https://www.newtownjets.com/pastSeasons/2007-news/2006GrandFinal.php/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2007===
Kenny-Dowall joined the [[Sydney Roosters]] and made his [[National Rugby League]] debut in round 1 the [[2007 NRL season]] against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], playing on the wing in the Roosters' 6–18 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/bunny-hop-with-pride/story-e6frexqr-1111113186090|title=Bunny hop With Pride|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> In round 4, against the [[Brisbane Broncos]], Kenny-Dowall scored his first NRL try in the Roosters 10–32 loss at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|SFS]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2007-04-06/broncos-open-account-with-win-over-roosters/2236330|title=Broncos open account with win over Roosters - ABC News (Australian Broadcasting Corporation)|newspaper=ABC News|date=6 April 2007|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> In round 13, Kenny-Dowall scored a hat-trick in the Roosters 64–30 victory over the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] at the SFS.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/news/lhqmatchreport/roosters-rack-up-cricket-score/2007/06/09/1181089395337.html|title=Roosters rack up cricket score|website=Smh.com.au|date=9 June 2007|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall finished his debut year in the NRL with playing in 14 matches and scored 8 tries in the 2007 NRL season. At the end of the season he was selected for the New Zealand All Golds to play against the Northern Union in October as part of the [[2007 All Golds Tour]]. He missed out on selection for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] for the 2007 [[Baskerville Shield]] test series against the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain Lions]] but was selected for the test against [[France national rugby league team|France]] due to the injury of [[Taniela Tuiaki]], scoring a try on test debut in the 22–14 win.{{cn|date=October 2022}}

===2008===
Kenny-Dowall played 19 matches and scored 15 tries for the Roosters in the 2008 NRL season. Kenny-Dowall was named in the [[New Zealand national rugby league team#2008 World Cup Squad|New Zealand training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup|2008 World Cup]] but was not selected. Instead, Kenny-Dowall played for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] which met the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous Dreamtime]] team in a curtain-raiser to the World Cup, scoring a try in the 34–26 loss.<ref>{{cite web|author=Steven Williams 6|url=http://leagueunlimited.com/news/aboriginal-side-too-good-for-maori/?redo=1|title=Aboriginal side too good for Maori|publisher=League Unlimited|date=2008-10-26|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref>

===2009===
Kenny-Dowall played in all 24 matches and scored 14 tries and kicked three goals in the Roosters' Wooden Spoon [[2009 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/the-wooden-spoon-to-premier-gold-roosters-run-recalls-league-history-20100921-15lfu.html|title=The wooden spoon to premier gold? Roosters run recalls league history|first=Chris|last=Barrett|date=21 September 2010|website=The Sydney Morning Herald}}</ref>

===2010===
In round 20, against the Brisbane Broncos, Kenny-Dowall scored 4 tries in the 34–30 win at [[Suncorp Stadium]],<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/sydney-roosters-hang-on-against-a-resilient-brisbane-broncos-to-win-34-30/story-fn2mcuj6-1225923260594|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> becoming the first Rooster in 35 years to score 4 tries in a single match. In the Roosters qualifying final match, against the [[Wests Tigers]] at the SFS, Kenny-Dowall scored the winning try in golden point extra time by intercepting the ball and running 70 metres downfield to win the match for the Roosters 19–15.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/shaun-kenny-dowall-scores-golden-point-try-as-roosters-beat-tigers-in-nrl-finals-thriller/story-e6frep5x-1225918720587|title=Shaun Keeny-Dowall Scores Golden Point Try as Roosters Beat Tigers in NRL Finals Thriller|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall played at centre in the Roosters [[2010 NRL Grand Final]] 32–8 loss to the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/st-george-illawarra-coach-wayne-bennett-says-grand-final-win-a-victory-for-true-believers/story-e6freuy9-1225933582360|title=Wayne Bennett Says Grand final Win a Victory for True Believers|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall played in all the Roosters 28 matches and scored 21 tries being the [[2010 NRL season]]'s highest tryscorer along with Newcastle's [[Akuila Uate]]. Kenny-Dowall cemented his position in the centres for the Kiwis in the [[2010 Rugby League Four Nations|2010 Four Nations]] series, scoring three tries including one in the final. Kenny-Dowall combined with [[Benji Marshall]], [[Jason Nightingale]] and [[Nathan Fien]] to set up the series winning 79th minute try.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/more-sports/kangaroos-crack-again-as-new-zealand-wins-four-nations-final-16-12/story-e6frf56c-1225953277821|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall's form throughout 2010 saw him runner up to Marshall in the [[Rugby League World Golden Boot Award]], where he made centre in the team of the year.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/sydney-roosters-centre-shaun-kenny-dowall-named-player-of-the-year-at-new-zealand-rugby-league-awards/story-fn488j1c-1225942108501|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref>

===2011===
On 13 February 2011, Kenny-Dowall was chosen to play for the [[NRL All Stars team]] on the interchange bench, scoring a try in the 28–12 win over the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] at [[Cbus Super Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-all-stars-defeat-the-indigenous-all-stars-28-12-at-skilled-park/story-e6frf3ou-1226005083135|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> On 10 March 2011, Kenny-Dowall agreed to a 4-year deal with the Roosters worth $1.4m.<ref>{{cite web|author=Rugby League|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/shaun-kennydowall-signs-bumper-new-deal-20110310-1bop4.html|title=Shaun Kenny-Dowall signs bumper new deal|website=Smh.com.au|date=2011-03-10|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall played for the New Zealand Kiwis at centre in the [[2011 Anzac Test]] at Cbus Super Stadium against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the 20–10 defeat.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/kangaroos/australia-play-new-zealand-in-the-mid-year-rugby-league-test-at-skilled-park-gold-coast/story-fn2mcuhw-1226051390439|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> In round 19, against the South Sydney Rabbitohs, Kenny-Dowall played his 100th NRL career match for the Roosters, scoring a try in the 21–20 loss at ANZ Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://m.nrl.com/rabbitohs-v-roosters-preview/tabid/10874/newsid/63769/default.aspx|title=Rabbitohs v Roosters preview|website=M.nrl.com|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/live-rabbitohs-v-roosters/story-e6frexnr-1226095872213|title=Rabbitohs v Roosters|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall played in 20 matches and scored 9 tries for the Roosters in the [[2011 NRL season]].

===2012===
Kenny-Dowall played in the Kiwis [[2012 Anzac Test]] at centre in the 20–12 loss to Australia at [[Eden Park]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/lowdown-on-test-blockbuster/story-e6frexni-1226334489834|title=Lowdown on Test Blockbuster|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall played in 17 matches and scored 8 tries for the Roosters in the [[2012 NRL season]].

===2013===
[[File:049 skddy.jpg|thumb|Shaun Kenny-Dowall with a run during the Roosters' inaugural Nines campaign]]
On 13 February 2013, Kenny-Dowall was chosen to play for the NRL All Stars team at centre in the 32–6 loss over the Indigenous All Stars at Suncorp Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/more-sports/live-indigenous-all-stars-v-nrl-all-stars-at-suncorp-stadium/story-e6frf56c-1226574515914|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> For the [[2013 Anzac Test]], Kenny-Dowall was selected to play for New Zealand at centre in their 32–12 loss to Australia at [[Canberra Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/australia-beats-new-zealand-32-12-at-canbera-stadium/story-e6frexnr-1226624627487|title=Australia Beat New Zealand 32-12|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall played in the Roosters 26–18 victory in the [[2013 NRL Grand Final]] against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]]. scoring a try in the 60th minute of the match. After the match it was revealed that he had broken a tooth and fractured his jaw in an early encounter in the match.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/roosters-grand-final-hero-shaun-kenny-dowall-played-75-minutes-with-broken-jaw/story-fni3gn3s-1226733887149|title=Roosters Grand Final Hero Shaun Kemny-Dowall Played 75 Minutes with Broken Jaw|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/nrl-grand-final-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/74996/default.aspx|title=NRL Grand Final player ratings|website=NRL.com|date=6 October 2013 |access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> The injury forced him to miss the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]]. Kenny-Dowall played in all the Roosters 27 matches and scored 9 tries.

===2014===
[[File:Shaun Kenny-Dowall.jpg|thumb|Kenny-Dowall with the ball against the Warriors in 2014]]

In the [[2014 NRL season]] round 1 season opener, against the South Sydney Rabbitohs, Kenny-Dowall played his 150th career NRL match for the Roosters in the 28–8 loss at ANZ Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/souths-run-roosters-ragged-in-28-8-whipping/tabid/10874/newsid/76615/default.aspx|title=Souths run Roosters ragged in 28-8 whipping|website=NRL.com|date=6 March 2014 |access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall finished off the 2014 NRL season with him playing in all of the Roosters 27 matches and scoring 11 tries. On 7 October 2014, Kenny-Dowall was selected in the New Zealand Kiwis final 24-man squad for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|2014 Four Nations]] series.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://m.nrl.com/four-rookies-in-kiwis-four-nations-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/82419/default.aspx|title=Four rookies in Kiwis Four Nations squad - NRL.com|website=M.nrl.com|access-date=2016-08-18|archive-date=23 July 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150723040509/http://m.nrl.com/four-rookies-in-kiwis-four-nations-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/82419/default.aspx|url-status=dead}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall played at centre in the Kiwis 22–18 Four Nations final win over Australia at [[Westpac Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/four-nations-2014-new-zealand-champions-after-defeating-australia-2218-in-wellington/story-fni3fbgz-1227124298670|title=New Zealand Champions After Defeating Australia 22-18|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref>

===2015===
In round 8, against the St George Illawarra Dragons, Kenny-Dowall scored his 100th NRL career try in the Roosters 14–12 loss at the Sydney Football Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/dragons-overcome-roosters-and-weather/tabid/10874/newsid/85681/default.aspx|title=Dragons overcome Roosters and weather|website=NRL.com|date=25 April 2015 |access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> On 3 May, Kenny-Dowall played for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the [[2015 Anzac Test]], playing at centre and scoring a try in the Kiwis' 26–12 win at Suncorp Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/new-zealand-kiwis-claim-third-straight-win-over-australian-kangaroos-for-first-time-since-1953-20150503-1mywnp.html|title=New Zealand Kiwis claim third straight win over Australian Kangaroos for first time since 1953|website=Smh.com.au|date=3 May 2015 |access-date=2015-12-20}}</ref> On 11 May 2015, Kenny-Dowall extended his contract with the Roosters for a further two years keeping him at the club until the end of the 2017 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/sydney-roosters/shaun-kennydowall-resigns-with-sydney-roosters-20150511-ggyrnn.html|title=Shaun Kenny-Dowall re-signs with Sydney Roosters|website=Smh.com.au|date=2015-05-11|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/68450617/shaun-kennydowall-resigns-with-sydney-roosters-for-two-more-years|title=Shaun Kenny-Dowall re-signs with Sydney Roosters for two more years|website=Stuff.co.nz|date=2015-05-11|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> In round 15, against the St George Illawarra Dragons, Kenny-Dowall scored 2 tries in the match becoming the Roosters second highest tryscorer with 105 tries, overtaking [[Bill Mullins]] record of 104 tries during the Roosters 19–14 win at the Sydney Football Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/roosters-hold-out-fast-finishing-dragons/tabid/10874/newsid/87318/default.aspx|title=Roosters hold out fast-finishing Dragons|website=NRL.com|date=22 June 2015 |access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/dragons-v-roosters-shaun-kenny-dowall-double-propels-his-side-to-win-over-gritty-st-george-illawarra/story-e6frf3vc-1227409945844|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref>  After missing nearly two months of the season due to off-field issues, Kenny-Dowall returned to the field in round 26 against the South Sydney Rabbitohs in the Roosters 30–0 win at the Sydney Football Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/sydney-roosters/shaun-kennydowall-set-for-roosters-return-against-south-sydney-rabbitohs-20150903-gjeaxm.html|title=Shaun Kenny-Dowall set for Roosters return against South Sydney Rabbitohs|website=Smh.com.au|date=2015-09-03|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/sydney-roosters-thrash-south-sydney-rabbitohs-to-clinch-nrl-minor-premiership-20150904-gjfnu0.html|title=Sydney Roosters thrash South Sydney Rabbitohs to clinch NRL minor premiership|website=Smh.com.au|date=4 September 2015 |access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> In the preliminary final match, against the Brisbane Broncos, Kenny-Dowall made headlines after making one of the biggest blunders of the season when he attempted throw a pass from the wing to fullback [[Roger Tuivasa-Sheck]] under pressure. The pass was easily intercepted by Broncos fullback [[Darius Boyd]] and Boyd ran away to score the try in the first minute of the match in the Roosters 31–12 loss at Suncorp Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/broncos-v-roosters-shaun-kenny-dowall-throws-a-crazy-pass-for-darius-boyd-to-score-intercept-try/story-fn2mcuj6-1227544318610|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/roosters-winger-shaun-kennydowalls-shocker-helps-brisbane-broncos-to-another-nrl-grand-final-20150925-gjvbgh.html|title=Roosters winger Shaun Kenny-Dowall's shocker helps Brisbane Broncos to another NRL grand final|website=Smh.com.au|date=25 September 2015 |access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall finished his highly publicised [[2015 NRL season]] with him being the Roosters highest tryscorer for the season with 17 tries in 20 matches. On 8 October 2015, Kenny-Dowall was selected in the 23-man New Zealand squad for the [[2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|2015 Tour of Great Britain]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/kiwis-name-squad-for-england-tour/tabid/10874/newsid/90473/default.aspx|title=Kiwis name squad for England tour|website=NRL.com|date=2015-10-08|access-date=2015-12-20}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall played in all three test matches against [[England national rugby league team|England]] on the wing and scored a try in the Kiwis 2-1 [[Baskerville Shield]] series loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/england-v-new-zealand-3rd-test-hosts-take-series-with-nervy-20-14-win-over-kiwis-in-wigan/story-fni3fbgz-1227609556526|title=England v New Zealand : Third Test Hosts Take Series With Nervy 20-14 Win Over Kiwis in Wigan|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2015-12-23}}</ref>

===2016===
On 2 February 2016, Kenny-Dowall was named in the Roosters [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|last=Luca|first=Rocco|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/roosters-unveil-2016-nines-squad/|title=Roosters unveil 2016 Nines squad|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2016-02-02|access-date=2016-08-18|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160323142600/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/roosters-unveil-2016-nines-squad/|archive-date=23 March 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref> On 29 February 2016, Kenny-Dowall was found not guilty to all of the domestic violence charges against his ex-girlfriend, Jessica Peris.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/news/nsw/shaun-kennydowall-not-guilty-of-assaulting-former-partner-jess-peris/news-story/e5078d98be4b4edf5542416cc1aad130?login=1|title=Shaun Kenny-Dowall Not Guilty of Assaulting Former Partner Jess Peris|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> In round 4, against the Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles, Kenny-Dowall played his 200th NRL career match and scored 2 tries in the Roosters 22–20 loss at the Sydney Football Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/roosters/shaun-kennydowall-celebrates-the-long-winding-road-to-200-nrl-games/news-story/eee8c47005bf3e054ae8d12cc20231ec|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=[[Fox Sports]]|date=23 March 2016 |access-date=2016-09-25}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2016-manly-sea-eagles-grind-out-tough-victory-to-keep-sydney-roosters-winless/news-story/22d0fb81ca9774b88ca18264b9a376d8|title=Manly Sea Eagles Grind Out Tough Victory to Keep Sydney Roosters Winless|website=Dailytelegrpah.com.au|access-date=2016-09-25}}</ref> On 1 May 2016, Kenny-Dowall was named in the New Zealand Kiwis 19-man squad for the [[2016 ANZAC Test]] but was later ruled out of the match due to a foot injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/kangaroos/live-new-zealand-announces-test-team-to-clash-with-kangaroos-in-transtasman-test/news-story/01b89e5afe027cc57635170040ab1e31|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=[[Fox Sports]]|date=May 2016 |access-date=2016-09-25}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/79674525/star-kiwis-centre-shaun-kennydowall-ruled-out-anzac-test|title=Star Kiwis centre Shaun Kenny-Dowall ruled out of Anzac test|website=Stuff.co.nz|date=2016-05-05|access-date=2016-09-25}}</ref> Kenny-Dowall finished the [[2016 NRL season]] with him playing in 19 matches and scoring 7 tries for the Roosters. On 6 September 2016, Kenny-Dowall was added to the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] train-on squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/warriors-dominate-kiwis-train-on-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/101077/default.aspx|title=Warriors dominate Kiwis train-on squad|website=nrl.com|date=6 September 2016 |access-date=5 May 2017}}</ref>

===2017===
On 5 May, Kenny-Dowall was charged with possessing a prohibited drug after he was found with half a gram of cocaine at a Sydney nightclub.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/sydney-roosters/roosters-star-shaun-kennydowall-charged-with-drug-possession-20170504-gvyyl2.html |title=Roosters star Shaun Kenny-Dowall charged with drug possession |publisher=Smh.com.au |date=2017-05-05 |access-date=2017-06-29}}</ref> On 12 June, he had his Roosters contract terminated.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/sydney-roosters-release-shaun-kenny-dowall-27808/ |title=Sydney Roosters release Shaun Kenny-Dowall |publisher=Zero Tackle |date=2017-06-13 |access-date=2017-06-27}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/roosters/sydney-roosters-officially-release-veteran-centre-shaun-kennydowall/news-story/4540573b7241b28f50a0cb4d5e7af764 |title=Sydney Roosters officially release veteran centre Shaun Kenny-Dowall |publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au |date=2017-06-13 |access-date=2017-06-29}}</ref> On 27 June, after escaping conviction for the charges, it was announced that he was able to join the [[Newcastle Knights]] effective immediately on a contract until the end of 2019.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/newcastle-knights-sign-shaun-kenny-dowall-28043/ |title=Newcastle Knights sign Shaun Kenny-Dowall |publisher=Zero Tackle |date=2017-02-18 |access-date=2017-06-27}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/knights/newcastle-knights-sign-shaun-kennydowall-but-injury-will-delay-former-roosters-return/news-story/802aeb159397f1ab50a9dba174ff0b0f |title=Newcastle Knights sign Shaun Kenny-Dowall but injury will delay former Rooster's return |publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au |date=2017-06-24 |access-date=2017-06-29}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/shaun-kennydowall-thanks-newcastle-knights-i-thought-my-nrl-career-was-over-20170628-gx0kb5.html |title=Shaun Kenny-Dowall vows to become a role model at Newcastle Knights |date=28 June 2017 |publisher=Smh.com.au |access-date=2017-06-29}}</ref> He finished his season with Roosters having played in 9 matches and scoring 2 tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTEtMTY5Ni00LS0tLS0tNTU1Mi0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0= |title=Custom Match List |publisher=Rugby League Project |access-date=2017-06-29}}</ref>

At the time he was released, he was the sixth highest capped Rooster in their 110-year history as well as their second highest try-scorer with 122. Kenny-Dowall also kicked three goals, all in 2009, for the tri-colours.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/teams/sydney-roosters/players.html?pos=&ord=app |title=Sydney Roosters - Players |publisher=Rugby League Project |access-date=2017-06-27}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/teams/sydney-roosters/players.html?pos=&ord=t |title=Sydney Roosters - Players |publisher=Rugby League Project |access-date=2017-06-27}}</ref>

Kenny-Dowall made his Knights debut against the Roosters in round 20 of the [[2017 NRL season|2017 season]], scoring the Knights' only try in their 4–28 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/roosters-ease-past-newcastle/tabid/10874/newsid/110221/default.aspx |title=Roosters ease past Newcastle |date=21 July 2017 |publisher=NRL.com |access-date=2017-07-21}}</ref>

===2018===
In round 9 of the [[2018 NRL season|2018 season]], Kenny-Dowall scored 2 tries in the Knights' 12–34 loss to the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], at [[Newcastle International Sports Centre|McDonald Jones Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/nrl-2018/round-9/newcastle-vs-south-sydney/summary.html|title=NRL 2018 - Round 9 - Rugby League Project|first=Shawn Dollin and Andrew|last=Ferguson|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=9 May 2018}}</ref>

===2019===
In September, it was revealed that 2019 would be Kenny-Dowall's last year in the NRL. On Thursday 19 September, it was announced that he would be joining [[Super League]] side [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on a 2-year contract starting from 2020.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/emotional-shaun-kennydowall-departs-knights-for-super-league/news-story/fd906d60b429683db742e7d635e69a11|title=NRL 2019: Shaun Kenny-Dowall, Newcastle Knights, UK Super League, Panthers vs Knights|date=6 September 2019}}</ref> He finished his time with the Knights having played in 53 matches and scoring 18 tries.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/shaun-kenny-dowall/summary.html|title=Shaun Kenny-Dowall - Career Stats & Summary - Rugby League Project|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref>

===2020===
Kenny-Dowall made 19 appearances for Hull KR and scored 4 tries in the 2020 Super League season.  Hull KR finished bottom of the table but avoided relegation due to the interrupted season which was caused by the [[COVID-19 pandemic]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54842864|title=Hull KR seek private equity investment to help secure club sale|work=BBC Sport |date=6 November 2020 }}</ref>

===2021===
In round 18 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored two tries for Hull KR in a 34-28 victory over Leigh.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58138787|title=Super League: Leigh Centurions 28-34 Hull Kingston Rovers - visitors edge 62-point thriller|work=BBC Sport |date=8 August 2021 }}</ref>
Kenny-Dowall played every game for Hull KR in the [[2021 Super League season]] as the club reached the semi-final stage of the competition before losing 28-10 against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/sep/30/catalans-dragons-hull-kr-super-league-play-off-semi-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=Catalans Dragons blast past Hull KR and into their first Super League Grand Final|newspaper=The Guardian|date=30 September 2021 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12415081/barrie-mcdermotts-2021-betfred-super-league-season-review/|title=Barrie McDermott's 2021 Betfred Super League season review|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/58663060.amp/|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 0-19 Hull KR - Robins set up a semi-final at Catalans|website=www.bbc.com|date=23 September 2021 }}</ref>

===2022===
Kenny-Dowall made 23 appearances for Hull KR in the 2022 Super League season scoring four tries. Kenny-Dowall was later named in the 2022 Super League dream team at centre.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/shaun-kenny-dowall-dream-team-7545055/|title=Everything Shaun Kenny-Dowall said on his Hull KR future and dream team inclusion|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>

===2023===
On 7 March, Kenny-Dowall was given a four-game ban including a £750 fine for making unnecessary contact with an injured opponent during Hull Kingston Rovers loss against [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in round 3 of the 2023 Super League competition.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64862814|title=Hull KR captain handed four-game ban for unnecessary contact|work=BBC Sport |date=6 March 2023 }}</ref>
On 24 May, Kenny-Dowall announced he would be retiring at the end of the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65690645|title=Shaun Kenny-Dowall: Hull KR centre to retire after 2023 season ends|work=BBC Sport |date=23 May 2023 }}</ref>
On 12 August, Kenny-Dowall played for Hull Kingston Rovers in their 17-16 golden point extra-time loss to [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]] in the Challenge Cup final.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|work=BBC Sport |date=12 August 2023 }}</ref>
Kenny-Dowall's final game as a player was their 42-12 semi-final loss against Wigan which saw Hull Kingston Rovers finish just one game away from the grand final.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/67008107|title=Relive Wigan's thumping win v Hull KR to head to Grand Final|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

==Controversies==

On 18 July 2015, Kenny-Dowall was charged with ten domestic violence offences over the alleged abuse of his former de facto partner, Jessica Peris, daughter of ex-Olympian-turned politician [[Nova Peris]].<ref name="dt">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/news/nsw/roosters-star-shaun-kenny-dowall-charged-with-domestic-violence-offences-against-former-partner-peris/story-fni0cx12-1227447047012|title=Shaun Kenny-Dowall Charged With Domestic Violence Offences Against Former Partner Jess Peris|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref> A police statement said the incidents were believed to have taken place between October 2014 and June 2015. Peris filed for an [[Apprehended Violence Order]] against Kenny-Dowall two days before he was charged.<ref name="dt"/> Kenny-Dowall pleaded not guilty to the charges at a hearing in August 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/sydney-roosters/roosters-centre-shaun-kennydowall-pleads-not-guilty-to-domestic-violence-charges-20150805-giso55.html|title=Roosters centre Shaun Kenny-Dowall pleads not guilty to domestic violence charges|website=Smh.com.au|date=2015-08-06|access-date=2016-08-18}}</ref>

Kenny-Dowall's trial commenced on 24 February 2016 in Sydney.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2016-02-25/shaun-kenny-dowall-roosters-jessica-peris-domestic-violence-case/7198508|title=Shaun Kenny-Dowall: Roosters told Jessica Peris 'to keep quiet' over domestic violence allegations|work=ABC News|date=25 February 2016|access-date=25 February 2016}}</ref> On 29 February, magistrate Gregory Grogin found him not guilty on all 11 charges, taking into account lack of evidence, witness credibility issues, and his good character.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2016-02-29/shaun-kenny-dowall-cleared-of-domestic-violence-charges/7206850|title=Shaun Kenny-Dowall: Sydney Roosters player cleared of domestic violence charges against Jessica Peris|work=ABC News|date=29 February 2016|access-date=29 February 2016}}</ref>

On 5 May 2017, Kenny-Dowall was charged with possessing a prohibited drug after he was found with half a gram of [[cocaine]] at a Sydney nightclub. He pled guilty and was placed on a one-year good behaviour bond. It was found in court that Kenny-Dowall had taken a hair follicle drug test which returned a negative result.<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.smh.com.au/national/nsw/exroosters-star-shaun-kennydowall-escapes-conviction-for-cocaine-possession-20170620-gwulbi.html| title = Ex-Roosters star Shaun Kenny-Dowall escapes conviction for cocaine possession| date = 20 June 2017}} </ref>
